body: Adding the dates for the record: forced two hours past shift on the chassis line Monday, Tuesday, and Wednesday last week. No one offered it to the low-seniority crew first like the contract says. I clocked the extra time but I never agreed to it.

Carlos, I've got it logged under the grievance committee. I pulled the overtime sign-up sheets for those three nights and you're right — the low-seniority list wasn't worked first. I'm drafting a Step 1 and I'll have you sign it this week. Stop by the hall Thursday between 4 and 6.
Appreciate it. I'll be there Thursday after my shift. Let me know if you need the clock-out records too — I kept screenshots.
The dues line on my stub this month was $4.20 higher than the posted rate. Just want to make sure it's not a payroll mistake before I let it go.

DeShawn, that's the scheduled dues adjustment the membership voted on at the December meeting — it took effect this pay period. The new rate is posted on the hall board and on local247aaw.org under Member Resources. Nothing wrong on your stub. Let me know if you want the breakdown.
Following up — press #4 is still running with the guard off as of this morning. I flagged it to my supervisor and nothing's moved. This is exactly the kind of thing that gets somebody hurt.

Clarence, thank you for getting this on record. I walked the floor with the plant safety officer this morning and we red-tagged press #4 — it's down until the guard is reinstalled and inspected. I logged it as a Tier 1 safety hold with the committee. You did the right thing reporting it.
Glad to hear it's tagged. The whole second shift will breathe easier. Thanks for moving fast on it.
Called the hall Tuesday about the body shop heat. Still no spot coolers fixed. Three of us got lightheaded again yesterday afternoon. We need this looked at before the committee meeting on the 24th.

Otis, I have it scheduled — the safety officer is doing temperature readings on the body-shop floor Thursday morning during peak heat. If they exceed the threshold in our health-and-safety language, we have grounds to require relief breaks and additional cooling. I'll bring the readings to the committee on the 24th.

The meeting is Thursday at 2 with my supervisor and HR over an attendance write-up. I have doctor's notes for two of the days they marked. I need a steward in the room with me — that's my right, correct?

Renee, yes — that's your Weingarten right, you're entitled to representation in any meeting that could lead to discipline. I'll be there Thursday at 2. Bring the doctor's notes and any text messages where you called in. Don't answer anything until I'm in the room. We'll meet 15 minutes before in the break area.
Thank you. I'll meet you at 1:45. That's a weight off — I was losing sleep over this.

The pinch point is right where the part feeds into weld cell 7. When the fixture cycles, your hand is inches from it if you're loading. We've all been nervous about it for weeks. Please get the safety officer on it.

Sondra, the safety officer inspected weld cell 7 this morning and agreed — the feed guard is inadequate for the new fixture. We've submitted a corrective action to engineering and put an interim two-hand control requirement in place so no one is loading with the cell live. I'll keep this open until the permanent guard is installed.
Checking on the Step 2 for my overtime grievance — do we have a hearing date? And are you the one representing me at the table?

Carlos, the Step 2 hearing is set for next Tuesday at 10 in the labor relations conference room. Yes, I'm representing you, with Carla Simmons sitting in from the committee. We've got the sign-up sheets and your clock records — it's a strong file. Be there by 9:45 so we can go over it.
The eyewash by the parts washer in the trades shop still doesn't work — the valve sticks and barely a trickle comes out. We handle solvents at that bench daily. This has been a week now.

Antoine, logged and red-tagged. I notified maintenance and the safety officer — an out-of-service eyewash where solvents are handled is a citable hazard, so it's a priority repair. In the meantime they've staged a portable eyewash at that bench. I'll confirm with you once the fixed unit passes the weekly flush test.
My health premium went up about eleven dollars this check and I'm not sure which tier I landed in. I never changed my coverage. Can you explain what moved?

Keisha, the plan renewed January 1 and the employee contribution went up modestly across all tiers under the new contract schedule. You're still in the same family tier you elected — nothing changed on your end. I can email you the side-by-side of last year's and this year's rates if that helps. The benefits office is open at the hall Tuesday and Thursday mornings if you'd rather go over it in person.
First time doing this. My supervisor changed my dock assignment without posting it and I think that violates the contract. What's the form and how long do I have to file?

Lorraine, you've got fourteen calendar days from when it happened to file, so you're well within the window. The grievance intake form is on local247aaw.org under Member Resources, or grab a paper copy at the hall front desk. Fill in the date, what happened, and the contract article if you know it — don't worry if you don't, a steward will help. Drop it with me and I'll route it to the grievance coordinator and get you a case number.
